top_signals.title: Micron Surges on AI Boom as Brent Oil Erases Iran War Gains | The Pulse 6/25/2026
top_signals.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Christian Keller, Barclays Head of Economics Research; John Kerry, Former US Secretary of State; Nils Schmid, German Deputy Defense Minister; Theo Baker, Author of 'How to Rule the World'  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Live markets: Bitcoin steadies ahead of U.S. PCE inflation data as DXY remains strong
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: UN-Led Hormuz Evacuation Scheme Moves 57 Ships Through Strait
top_signals.summary: Some 57 ships carrying an estimated 1,100 seafarers have transited the Strait of Hormuz since June 23 under a United Nations evacuation plan launched this week, data from the U.N.'s shipping agency showed on Thursday.These are the first numbers to be released by the U.N.'s International Maritime Org
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: China Begins €5 Billion Bond Sale, Largest Ever in Euros
top_signals.summary: China kicked off marketing up to €5 billion ($5.7 billion) of sovereign bonds in what could be its largest-ever such deal in euros.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
top_signals.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
